behaviors.description: The test prompt used for behavioral verification does not embed phase-specific instructions copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: The failure usually happens at prompt assembly boundaries (template + test harness + fixtures), not inside a single pure function. A unit test can miss copied instructions introduced by higher-level composition.
behaviors.test_description: Build the full behavioral-test prompt through the real test harness, then assert it does not contain canonical phase-instruction snippets from SKILL.md (or their normalized forms). Fail if any copied section appears in the final assembled prompt.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a wiring/infrastructure dependency on real file-backed skill context. In-process mocks can hide hard-coded fallback logic and still pass.
behaviors.test_description: Run the behavioral test using real plugin/skill files in a temp workspace. Inject a unique sentinel instruction only in SKILL.md and verify the invocation context includes it, while also asserting no equivalent hard-coded rule exists in the test prompt template path.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill context regresses. Mocked or deterministic substitutes can miss this causal dependency and hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same user-style prompt twice against the real model: once with intact SKILL.md and once with the targeted section removed/degraded. Keep all other inputs fixed. Assert pass on intact skill and fail on degraded skill under the same behavioral assertions.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is ordering plus filesystem artifact persistence across modules (invocation, capture, parser). Unit tests often miss cross-module sequencing regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test invocation where parser/extractor intentionally throws on malformed JSON. Assert a raw-output artifact file is created and contains the unmodified model text even when parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in the assertion pipeline (output -> extraction -> schema/shape checks). Keyword-only checks can pass on echoed text; structured assertions must be validated in-context.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative model outputs through the real assertion path: one output that contains expected keywords but wrong structure, and one with correct structure. Assert the first fails and the second passes based on schema/field-position checks.
